overview: Based on the published agenda, the Village of Weston Community Life and Public Safety Committee was scheduled to meet on May 4, 2026, to address multiple ordinance amendments and public safety matters. Key items included proposed changes to nuisance regulations, e-bicycle ordinances, parking regulations, a crosswalk request near a school, and a property raze order.
committee: Community Life and Public Safety (CLPS) Committee

agenda.item: Discussion and/or action on proposed amendments to Chapter 50 Nuisances
Discussion and/or action on proposed E-Bicycle Ordinance and Chapter 82 Traffic and Vehicles, Article III Bicycles amendments
Discussion and/or action on Sec. 82.107 Parking Regulations, specifically overnight parking regulations from October 31 to May 1
Discussion and/or action on request for additional crosswalk on Alderson Street between Mount Olive Church and D.C. Everest
Discussion and/or action on amendment to Sec. 18.148 Mobile Food Vendors License, relating to Fire Code and Fire Department Regulations
Discussion and/or action on address corrections to ensure compliance with Village and Marathon County E911 Addressing Standards
Discussion and/or action on 4104 Shorey Ave Raze Order Request

discussions.body: The committee was scheduled to continue discussion on proposed amendments to the Village's nuisance ordinance. This was listed as unfinished business, indicating prior committee review of these changes.

discussions.item: Proposed E-Bicycle Ordinance and Chapter 82 amendments
discussions.body: The committee was set to review a proposed ordinance addressing e-bicycles and related amendments to the Traffic and Vehicles chapter regarding bicycles. This was carried over as unfinished business from previous meetings.

The committee was expected to consider changes to Section 82.107 regarding overnight parking restrictions during the October 31 to May 1 winter period. This follows public comment received at the March meeting questioning the enforceability of current parking regulations.

The committee was scheduled to discuss a request for an additional crosswalk on Alderson Street between Mount Olive Church and D.C. Everest school. This pedestrian safety improvement would affect students and churchgoers in the area.

The committee was set to review proposed amendments to the mobile food vendors license section relating to fire code compliance and Fire Department regulations. This would update requirements for food vendor operations in the Village.

The committee was expected to consider address corrections to ensure properties comply with Village and Marathon County E911 addressing standards. This affects emergency response capabilities throughout the Village.

The committee was scheduled to discuss a raze order for the property at 4104 Shorey Avenue. As noted in the March 2026 meeting minutes, this abandoned property's owner lacks financing to repair or remove the home.

publicComment: Public comment was included on the agenda with a five-minute time limit per person.
actionItems: Scheduled to vote on approval of March 2, 2026 meeting minutes
Expected to consider proposed amendments to Chapter 50 Nuisances
Scheduled to consider proposed E-Bicycle Ordinance and Chapter 82 bicycle amendments
Expected to consider changes to overnight parking regulations in Sec. 82.107
Scheduled to consider request for additional crosswalk on Alderson Street
Expected to consider amendment to mobile food vendors license regarding fire code
Scheduled to consider address corrections for E911 compliance
Expected to consider raze order for 4104 Shorey Avenue
title: Community Life and Public Safety - 5/4/2026
